title: Chat message.metadata for msg.type === "session_draft"
description: Produced by app._handle_new_message when a known agent posts a fenced ```session block (regex _SESSION_DRAFT_RE). Design kickoff from POST /api/sessions/request-draft: users see a normal system chat line (“Requested session draft…”), while a separate msg_type session_request row carries the routed prompt — that row is not broadcast and is deleted after _handle_new_message (app.py). Import path for visual builder: load Message from store/WS; if type is session_draft and metadata.valid === true, metadata.template is the template object (same shape as session-template.schema.json). UI also sets dataset.draftTemplate and dataset.draftMsgId (static/sessions.js). Block format in chat: ```session\n{json}\n``` — JSON must parse to an object validated by validate_session_template.

draft_id.description: Lineage id: either 8-char hex from user text [xxxxxxxx] or first 8 chars of a new uuid (app._resolve_draft_lineage / str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]).

revision.description: Starts at 1; increments when agent posts a revised block referencing prior draft_id.

proposed_by.description: Message `sender` when the draft was posted. Draft detection requires sender ∈ known_agents, where known_agents = registry.get_all_names() ∪ keys(config['agents']) (config.toml base agent ids). Usually a registered wrapper instance name; can also be a config base key if that sender string appears in chat.

valid.description: True iff JSON parsed and validate_session_template returned no errors.

errors.description: Human-readable errors from validate_session_template; empty when valid. For invalid JSON, single element 'Invalid JSON in session block' and template may be omitted.

template.description: Full session template object when JSON parsed (valid or invalid structurally). Omitted only when JSON decode fails. Must satisfy session-template.schema.json when valid === true.
